next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 17

On the tram to WISE, Anya could tell Berlint had started thinking about other things.

The air still wanted a coat and gloves, but the thoughts didn't. All winter the city had been thinking about arrests, investigations, borders, who would take the fall and who would get paid to bury the embarrassing parts. Now the noise had thinned into ordinary stuff: paint samples, grocery lists, a man worrying about his mother's hip surgery, somebody trying to remember whether the butcher closed at six. The Renewal Front had gone where scandals went after the shouting, into courtrooms and committees and procedural language. Both governments were doing accountability in public and burial work in private. Damian still showed up in the papers, but in the tired middle now, in consultations and threats and reform panels. He was not a hero and not a disgrace. He was inconveniently real.

Inside WISE, the restructuring showed. Falk's desk in the analyst pool had been cleared within days of his arrest, but the gap still held its shape. There were new faces and new security checks. One junior officer at the checkpoint was comparing signatures as if ink itself had turned treasonous. Sylvia's review board had gone through the place hard enough that even the coffee tasted more cautious.

Sylvia had a file open on her desk when Anya came in. She closed it before Anya could read the label. The thought on the surface was quick and sour: *record. gaps. should've seen it sooner.*

"Sit down."

Anya sat.

"The review board has completed its assessment of your record. Your field status is re...
open_hooks_to_carry: Anya tells Damian she is a telepath — not strategically, but because she cannot build something real on asymmetry
Damian's reaction arc: anger, horror at the scope, then the complicated thing underneath
Anya does not defend herself — she sits with earned anger
Damian's counter-revelation: he has known she is Anya Forger since the gala — he confirmed it, chose to continue the alliance, met her family knowing everything except the telepathy
The asymmetry inverts and collapses: she read his mind without consent, he knew her identity without disclosure — they both operated inside knowledge the other didn't know they had
The core vulnerability: Anya does not know which of her feelings for him are hers and which are absorbed from his
He needs time. Before he leaves, he says one thing knowing she will hear the thought behind it. The thought matches.

payoffs_to_land.description: The moral asymmetry seeded in Ch 3 and crystallized in Ch 9 is finally confronted — but doubled by Damian's counter-revelation that he also held hidden knowledge
Damian's recognition, which built from Ch 2 through Ch 12 and was confirmed in Ch 13, is disclosed to Anya — completing the thread
The Eden Academy photo from Ch 7 resonates — Damian kept it, used it to confirm her identity, and now both know

prev_tail_excerpt:
...n the report because Damian was useful and frightened and not what WISE thought he was. She had written it because she wanted room to move. She had written it because the first lie had worked and competence made the second lie easier. After that the reasons had piled up until they looked like structure.

None of them put Crane back anywhere.

Loid came out a few minutes later with his belt still missing and her folded note in one hand. He stopped beside her, not close enough to touch.

"Can you walk," he asked.

She nodded.

He looked at her for a long second, the way he looked at doors before deciding whether they were safe to open.

"Good," he said. "You're with me until we're home."

"Dad, I..."

"Not here." His voice stayed level. "And not in pieces. All of it."

That was worse than if he had shouted.

He folded the note once more and slid it into his pocket. His hand lifted as if he meant to steady her shoulder, stopped before contact, and dropped again.

Out by the barricades a producer was already saying Damian's surname into a microphone while the cameraman beside him thought *traitor* and the woman on his other side thought *hero* and none of them sounded certain even in their own heads. Above them, somewhere out of sight, camera six still held whatever it had recorded on the south doors. Falk was in custody. Voss was exposed without being simplified. Yuri was living inside a lie he had chosen.

Loid turned toward the street. Anya fell into step with him automatically.

He kept her close enough to stop if she drifted and far enough that their sleeves never touched.
state_deltas_required: the moral weight of Anya's lifelong secret AND Damian's counter-knowledge — both have been deceiving each other

Both secrets are out. Anya discloses her telepathy; Damian reveals he has known her identity since the gala. The relationship is remade on genuinely mutual terms — both deceived, both chose each other anyway.
